gap_statement: Current coverage appears to underrepresent Chinese-language or Chinese-context studies.
counter_evidence: 7 papers explicitly study Chinese contexts: Xi'an (papers 03_2006, 06_2013), Dalian (08_2016), Hefei (09_2018, 12_2016), Hong Kong (10_2020), Shanghai (14_2020)
All Chinese-context papers are English-language publications, but the geographic coverage of Chinese pedestrian behavior is substantial

gap_statement: There are unresolved claim differences across papers under the same task, suggesting a need for clearer comparison criteria.
counter_evidence: Task distribution shows diverse specific tasks; multiple papers addressing 'pedestrian_signal_non_compliance_decision_modeling' (papers 03_2006, 06_2013, 10_2020, 12_2016) show complementary rather than contradictory findings (all find high violation rates, identify personal/environmental factors)
Methodological differences across papers (simulation vs. survey vs. observation) explain differing results rather than true contradictions
Geographic/contextual differences (Xi'an, Dalian, Hefei, Hong Kong) account for varying factor importance - these are not contradictions but context-dependent findings
The 32 contradictions may reflect expected scholarly discourse rather than unresolved conflicts
